architecture_fit.note: Single attention head, no MLP/unembed — minimal delta from base_model; selects window via real Q·K softmax and aggregates token values.

baseline_comparison.note: Constant-predictor baseline plotted alongside; ablating selection collapses exactly onto it, making the win explicit.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built circuit, declared as such; two ablations (no-selection, no-scaling) show both pieces are load-bearing.

operating_range.note: Exact (MSE=0) across the full k=2..32 sweep; robustness=1.0, no degradation as window grows.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism written by hand as float32 CUDA tensors; reproduces target exactly to fp precision.

visual_judgement.note: MSE-vs-k log-log chart with ablations + baseline, plus interactive attention/values inspector — claim is legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties chart grain to the goal's question (degradation vs window length) and justifies baseline + ablation curves.

notes: A clean hand-built single-head range-sum circuit that selects the window with a genuine Q·K softmax over one-hot positional keys and recovers the sum via a length-scaled readout, achieving exact MSE (0.0) and robustness 1.0 across the full k=2..32 sweep. The two ablations and constant baseline make the claim falsifiable, and the visualisation (MSE-vs-k plus an interactive attention inspector) is well matched to the goal. All scored rubric items are 5, so the mean is 5 → perfect.
